name: Meñaka y Gatika – circular desde Mungia
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Mungia are set within a sheltered valley in Bizkaia, Basque Country, characterized by rolling hills and the Butroi river valley. The landscape features significant mountains such as Sollube (684 m) and Jata (592 m), offering varied terrain for cyclists. Historical country houses and Romanesque bridges are also present throughout the rural scenery. This region provides diverse cycling opportunities across different landscapes. Best touring cycling routes around Mungia The most popular touring cycling route is Meñaka y Gatika – circular desde Mungia, an 18.6 miles (29.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 53 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores rural landscapes and traditional farmsteads. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Paso de Aretxabalagana – circular desde Mungia por Larrabezúa, a difficult 25.5 miles (41.0 km) path. This route offers challenging climbs through varied terrain, including significant elevation gain. Local touring cyclists also love the Jata Radar Station loop from Mungia, a 20.2 miles (32.5 km) trail leading through the area around Jata Mountain, often completed in about 2 hours 39 minutes. Touring cycling around Mungia is defined by rolling hills, the Butroi river valley, and mountains like Sollube and Jata. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more difficult ascents. The routes in Mungia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from a few reviews. More than 100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Mungia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Mungia?
A: There are over 20 touring cycling routes around Mungia, offering diverse options for exploring the region's landscapes. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate rides to more challenging ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes near Mungia?
A: The terrain around Mungia is characterized by rolling hills and the valley of the Butroi river. You'll also encounter more significant mountains nearby, such as Sollube (684 m) and Jata (592 m), providing varied landscapes from rural paths to more challenging climbs.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Mungia?
A: While many routes involve rolling hills, the Atxuri Greenway, starting near Uriguen park, offers a more accessible and leisurely option suitable for families. It provides a pleasant ride through the local scenery.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural features I can see along the cycling routes?
A: Mungia's routes often pass by historical country houses (baserri), Romanesque bridges, and water mills. For specific natural and historical highlights, consider exploring areas near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/275684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint John of Gaztelugatxe&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1516121" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Butrón Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoying the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5269209" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views over the Urdaibai marsh&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes starting from Mungia?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as circular loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079126509/menaka-y-gatika-circular-desde-mungia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Meñaka y Gatika – circular desde Mungia&lt;/a&gt; route offers an immersive experience through authentic Biscay landscapes. Another option is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068553747/paso-de-aretxabalagana-circular-desde-mungia-por-larrabezua" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paso de Aretxabalagana – circular desde Mungia por Larrabezúa&lt;/a&gt;, known for its significant elevation gain.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Mungia?
A: Mungia, located in a sheltered valley, generally offers pleasant conditions for cycling. Spring and autumn typically provide mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, making them ideal for exploring the region. Summer can also be enjoyable, though it may be warmer.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Mungia?
A: Mungia, as a local municipality, offers various public parking options within the town center and near popular starting points for routes. Specific parking details for individual routes are often available on their respective komoot tour pages.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Mungia?
A: The touring cycling routes in Mungia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from rolling hills to mountain ascents, and the picturesque rural scenery dotted with historical elements.

Q: Are there any routes that offer significant viewpoints or panoramic vistas?
A: Absolutely. Routes that ascend mountains like Jata and Sollube provide rewarding views. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31961453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jata Radar Station loop from Mungia&lt;/a&gt; leads through the area around Jata Mountain, offering great perspectives. You can also find specific viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4006662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from San Pedro de Atxarre&lt;/a&gt; in the wider region.

Q: How long do the touring cycling routes around Mungia typically take to complete?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. For instance, a moderate route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079126509/menaka-y-gatika-circular-desde-mungia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Meñaka y Gatika – circular desde Mungia&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 29.9 km (18.6 miles), takes approximately 1 hour 53 minutes. More difficult and longer routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40329122" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guernica Mural in Gernika – Mundaka Harbor loop from Belako&lt;/a&gt; (52.4 km / 32.6 miles), can take over 3 hours.

Q: Can I connect to other towns or regions from Mungia by touring bike?
A: Yes, Mungia's strategic location allows for connections to the wider Basque Country. Routes such as 'Mungia - Bilbao' (not listed in this guide) and 'Mungia-Butron-Lemoiz-Mungia' (not listed in this guide) enable exploration of nearby towns and offer potential for longer cycling tours, including coastal areas.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Mungia offers several difficult routes with significant elevation gains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31878830" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Summit of Sollube – Sollube Summit loop from Mungia&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 34 km (21.1 miles) route that takes you to the summit, providing a demanding ride for experienced cyclists.
tips: Nice hermitage that is accessed by a pleasant and quite popular bike path. It has a fountain and a porch with a table to take shelter in case of rain.
In my opinion, together with Gautegiz de Arteaga, they are the two most important castles in Bizkaia
